content: The generic Self-Refine framework for unstructured LLM output refinement can be extended by integrating multiple inconsistent perspective sampling from existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break the local optimum trap that plagues single-perspective iterative self-feedback. This creates a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data, while fixing its common failure mode of repeating the same subtle error across iterations.
